title: baillonella sylvestris
intro: Baillonella sylvestris, also known as the African mahogany or okoume, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a large, deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of central and western Africa, valued for its timber and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its various health benefits. Baillonella sylvestris is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
